introSection: Trailblazer is a steel terrain roller coaster located at Hersheypark in Hershey, Pennsylvania, USA. It is the park's second oldest standing roller coaster after Comet.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Hersheypark purchased Trailblazer for $975,000 on April 30, 1973.

Trailblazer opened on May 18, 1974. It is located in the Pioneer Frontier section of the park. The coaster features one lift hill, a helix that circles four times, and a brake run level with the launch section of Storm Runner.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The Arrow Dynamics trains were replaced with Premier Rides built ones in 2003. These new trains featured individual lap bars. One of the original cars is now on display at the National Roller Coaster Museum.

fullText:
Trailblazer is a steel terrain roller coaster located at Hersheypark in Hershey, Pennsylvania, USA. It is the park's second oldest standing roller coaster after Comet.

Hersheypark purchased Trailblazer for $975,000 on April 30, 1973.

Trailblazer opened on May 18, 1974. It is located in the Pioneer Frontier section of the park. The coaster features one lift hill, a helix that circles four times, and a brake run level with the launch section of Storm Runner.



Black track and red supports.

The Arrow Dynamics trains were replaced with Premier Rides built ones in 2003. These new trains featured individual lap bars. One of the original cars is now on display at the National Roller Coaster Museum.
